libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When testing com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) overloads without an explicit Set, set the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the fully qualified test ObjectFactory class name(s), comma-separated. Restore/clear the previous property value in finally/AfterEach to avoid cross-test leakage. Alternatively, call the overload that accepts an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set containing the test factory class. Avoid remote factory class locations unless deliberately testing them; those require com.mchange.v2.naming.supportReferenceRemoteFactoryClassLocation=true and are security-sensitive.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The whitelist is a JVM-wide system property, so generated tests can become order-dependent if they do not restore it.
POM dependencies for Typesafe Config, log4j, log4j2, and slf4j are optional integrations, not required setup for core library coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 adds a mandatory JNDI ObjectFactory whitelist for ReferenceableUtils overloads that do not take an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set; existing meaningful coverage of ReferenceableUtils must configure that whitelist or use the explicit overload.
